phases.goal: Build a reliable study system and eliminate friction

steps.title: Audit your current study habits
steps.description: Track how you currently spend your study time for 5 days. Note start/end times, what you actually studied, and how focused you felt (1–5). Most people discover they study far less effectively than they think.
steps.success_metric: 5 days of honest study-time tracking completed

steps.title: Set up a dedicated study environment
steps.description: Choose one physical space used only for focused study. Remove your phone or use an app blocker. A consistent environment primes your brain to focus before you even start.
steps.success_metric: Dedicated study space set up and used for 5 sessions

steps.title: Adopt the Pomodoro technique
steps.description: Study in 25-minute focused blocks with 5-minute breaks. After 4 blocks, take a 20-minute break. This matches your brain's natural attention cycles and prevents burnout.

phases.goal: Replace passive review with active recall and spaced repetition

steps.title: Switch to active recall
steps.description: Stop re-reading notes. Instead, close your notes and try to recall the key concepts from memory. Write them down, then check. This retrieval practice doubles long-term retention compared to passive review.
steps.success_metric: Active recall used in every study session for 4 weeks

steps.title: Build a spaced repetition system
steps.description: Use Anki or similar flashcard software for any factual content (vocabulary, formulas, dates). Set up daily 15-minute review sessions. Spaced repetition is the most evidence-backed learning technique that exists.
steps.success_metric: Anki deck built with 100+ cards and reviewed daily for 4 weeks

steps.title: Use the Feynman technique for complex topics
steps.description: For any concept you struggle with: explain it in simple language as if teaching a child. Where you can't, that's your gap. Go back to source material and try again. Understanding beats memorisation.
steps.success_metric: Feynman explanations written for every major topic

phases.goal: Optimise focus, energy, and exam performance

steps.title: Protect sleep and exercise
steps.description: Pulling all-nighters cuts memory consolidation by up to 40%. Aim for 7–8 hours. Add 3x weekly exercise — even 20-minute walks improve focus and memory retention measurably.
steps.success_metric: Sleeping 7+ hours and exercising 3x/week for 3 consecutive weeks

steps.title: Practice under exam conditions
steps.description: At least 2 weeks before any exam, do timed practice under real conditions — no notes, no phone, same duration. Familiarity with the format reduces anxiety and surfaces hidden gaps.
steps.success_metric: At least 3 full timed practice sessions completed

milestones: Sleep and exercise routine locked in
